title: Bayes' Rule
description: Learn to invert conditional probabilities: turn P(positive | disease) into P(disease | positive). From medical screening to spam filters to scientific inference — Bayesian updating as a universal reasoning engine.
persona: For anyone who reads statistics in the news and wants to think clearly about evidence. No prior probability assumed beyond basic fractions.
tagline: Update your beliefs when new evidence arrives.

lessons.title: The Update Rule
lessons.summary: Two coins, one flip: a head pushes 1/2 → 2/3. Evidence rescales each hypothesis by how well it predicted the evidence.

lessons.title: The Base-Rate Trap
lessons.summary: A 99% test on a 1-in-100 disease still leaves you at 50% — the prior is a heavy anchor strong evidence must overcome.

lessons.title: Stacking Evidence
lessons.summary: Independent evidence multiplies: today's posterior is tomorrow's prior, so repeated clues compound to near-certainty.

lessons.title: Which Hypothesis?
lessons.summary: With three machines and one defective part, share alone doesn't decide — share × rate does. The two-hypothesis rule is the same rule for N.

lessons.title: The Host's Clue
lessons.summary: The host's action is the evidence, and its likelihood comes from his rules. Forcing pushes the switch door to 2/3.

lessons.title: The Question Behind the Clue
lessons.summary: The update is set by the exact event you condition on — write the clue as a subset of outcomes and the math is forced.

lessons.title: Reading Evidence Backwards
lessons.summary: P(evidence | hypothesis) is not P(hypothesis | evidence). The prosecutor's fallacy: flip the conditional with Bayes and restore the prior.

lessons.title: Base Rates in the Wild
lessons.summary: Real judgments hide the base rate — the number you look up. Name it, then update: same rule, every costume.
